name: Carnival of Venice
traditions: Elaborate masked balls and masquerades; ornate Venetian masks (bauta, moretta, medico della peste); gondola parades; costume contests.
historicalBackground: Venice Carnival dates to 1162, celebrating a military victory. The tradition of masks allowed social classes to mingle anonymously. Revived in 1979.
description: One of the world's oldest and most elegant carnivals, famous for its ornate masks and mysterious masquerade balls on Venice's canals.
